title: Aureline extension manifest, effective-permission, and publisher-identity baseline (first ecosystem-bearing lane)
description: Versioned cross-tool boundary for the extension-manifest baseline, the effective-permission baseline summary, and the install / review decision record on the first ecosystem-bearing lane. The eventual extension-manifest crate's Rust types are the source of truth (see crates/aureline-extensions/src/manifest_baseline/); this schema is the typed boundary every non-owning surface (install / review docs, support exports, runtime truth badges, CI / schema validation) reads. Adding a new member to any enumerated set, or an additive optional field, is additive-minor with an extension_manifest_baseline_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row. Anonymous or ambient publisher privilege is not acceptable on any record; a publisher_identity_ref MUST be present on every manifest baseline row. Raw artifact bytes, raw signing-key material, raw policy-bundle bytes, and raw publisher-private data MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs only.

extension_manifest_baseline_schema_versi.description: Integer schema version for this extension-manifest baseline export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

publisher_trust_tier_class.description: Frozen publisher-trust-tier vocabulary on the M1 lane. 'anonymous_publisher_class' is a typed terminal class so a row that fails to attribute a publisher cannot pretend to be unverified; it is admitted only for the explicit denial drill and MUST be paired with install_decision_class = denied and install_decision_reason_class = publisher_anonymous.

publisher_lifecycle_state_class.description: Frozen publisher-lifecycle-state vocabulary on the M1 lane. A retired or quarantined publisher MUST NOT carry install_decision_class admit / admit_with_step_up.

extension_lifecycle_state_class.description: Frozen extension-lifecycle-state vocabulary on the M1 lane. A retired or quarantined extension MUST NOT carry install_decision_class admit / admit_with_step_up.

manifest_origin_source_class.description: Frozen manifest-origin-source vocabulary. 'unknown_source_class' is a terminal class so a row whose origin cannot be attributed cannot quietly impersonate the public registry; it is admitted only for the explicit denial drill and MUST be paired with install_decision_class = denied.

host_contract_family_class.description: Frozen host-contract-family vocabulary on the M1 lane. Mirrors the broader ADR-0012 host-contract vocabulary so the M1 baseline does not fork it.

permission_scope_class.description: Minimum permission-scope vocabulary recognised on the M1 lane. Mirrors the ADR-0012 permission-scope vocabulary so the M1 baseline does not fork it. Adding a new scope is additive-minor with an extension_manifest_baseline_schema_version bump; repurposing a scope is breaking.

scope_target.description: Typed target for the scope. Mirrors the ADR-0012 scope_target shape (path prefix, provider id, setting id, capability ref, host-contract ref, subscription selector, command id, or credential-handle alias class).

scope_constraint.description: Optional narrowing detail. Examples: 'read-only', 'egress to declared hosts only', 'requires step-up', 'bounded to declared setting scope'. Free-form but never raw secret material.

rationale_label.description: Human-legible intent for the scope. The user / admin install / review surface MUST render this rationale; rows that ship a permission with no rationale_label MUST be denied with install_decision_reason_class = declared_permission_rationale_required.

effective_permission_diff_class.description: Typed diff class per scope entry between the declared and effective permission sets. 'unchanged' means the declared entry survived; 'narrowed' means the entry was reduced (e.g. a path prefix trimmed); 'denied' means the entry was dropped; 'step_up_required' means the entry survives only behind step-up approval; 'widening_attempted_blocked' means the row attempted to claim a permission not in the declared manifest set and the effective-permission truth blocked it. The install / review surface MUST surface widening_attempted_blocked as a denial.

narrowing_reason_label.description: Human-legible reason the effective entry differs from the declared entry. Examples: 'unchanged', 'policy floor narrows write to step-up', 'policy pack denied egress', 'declared scope did not include this scope; widening blocked'. Free-form but never raw bundle bytes.

manifest_scope_completeness_class.description: Self-declared completeness class for the manifest-baseline row. 'complete' is the only class that may be paired with install_decision_class admit; every other class MUST be paired with a denial install decision so install / review never silently runs on an incomplete manifest.

install_decision_class.description: Typed install / review decision the install surface emits for the manifest baseline row. 'admit' is the only class that may run the extension immediately; 'admit_with_step_up' admits only after a typed step-up approval; 'review_only' lands the manifest for review without enabling it; 'denied' refuses install and is the required outcome for incomplete manifests, anonymous publishers, quarantined publishers, and effective-permission widening attempts.

install_decision_reason_class.description: Frozen denial / admit reason vocabulary on the M1 install / review decision. The install surface MUST emit a typed reason for every decision; a generic 'unavailable' affordance is forbidden.

summary_freshness_class.description: Mirrors the ADR-0011 freshness-class vocabulary. A summary whose canonical owner could not be contacted within its refresh window MUST NOT render authoritative_live.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class for the record when emitted to an observable sink. Mirrors the ADR-0007 redaction defaults plus 'metadata_safe_default' for surfaces that only export typed enum tokens and opaque refs.

manifest_baseline_id.description: Stable id for this manifest-baseline row, namespaced under 'manifest_baseline:'.

extension_identity.description: Opaque namespaced id of the form 'publisher_id/extension_id'. Never reused after retirement.

extension_version.description: SemVer string. Pinning and floors are policy-pack concerns; this field carries the declared value only.

origin_source_label.description: Human-legible origin label. Examples: 'public registry: registry.aureline.dev', 'private registry: acme.example', 'offline bundle: airlock-2026-q2.bundle'. The install / review surface MUST render this label.

publisher_identity_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publisher identity record. MUST be a non-empty ref; anonymous or ambient publisher privilege is not acceptable. The 'anonymous_publisher_class' trust tier is permitted only on a denial-drill row whose install decision is denied with reason publisher_anonymous.

publisher_display_label.description: Human-legible publisher label. Never the trust authority on its own.

publisher_signing_key_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publisher's currently active signing-key record. Raw key material never carried.

declared_permissions.description: Declared permission-scope entries. Every entry MUST carry a non-empty rationale_label.

manifest_baseline_ref.description: Ref back to the manifest-baseline row this summary was computed against.

effective_permissions.description: Effective permission-scope entries after policy-pack narrowing and widening blocks. Every effective entry's scope_class + scope_target MUST appear in the declared set; an entry that does not is recorded in declared_vs_effective_diff with diff_class widening_attempted_blocked and dropped from effective_permissions.

declared_vs_effective_diff.description: Typed diff per scope entry between declared and effective. A review surface that hides this field MUST be denied install_decision_class admit.

widening_attempted_blocked_count.description: Count of declared_vs_effective_diff entries with diff_class widening_attempted_blocked. Equality with the number of widening_attempted_blocked entries in declared_vs_effective_diff MUST hold; a widening_attempted_blocked_count > 0 MUST be paired with install_decision_class = denied and install_decision_reason_class = effective_permission_widening_attempted.

applied_policy_pack_refs.description: Refs to policy-pack constraint rows that applied. Policy-pack content is never embedded; only refs.

decision_summary.description: Human-legible summary the install / review surface renders next to the typed decision. Examples: 'Admitted: verified publisher and complete manifest.', 'Denied: publisher quarantined.', 'Denied: requested filesystem_write was not declared in the manifest.'.
